title: Agent Entry Surface Standard
purpose: Govern how durable system knowledge up-propagates into AGENTS.override.md, AGENTS.md, CODEX.md, and CLAUDE.md without bloating those entry surfaces.

known_principle.statement: New durable system capabilities appear in agent entry surfaces as compressed route pointers, not copied protocol bodies.
known_principle.authority_posture: standard-governed principle statement, not raw-seed voice
known_principle.operational_test: A cold agent can see that the capability exists, route to the owning substrate, and verify freshness without the adapter embedding the full doctrine.

entry_adoption_contract.principle_title: Live Projections Must Be Entry Routes
entry_adoption_contract.rule: A durable capability, projection, evaluator, provider lane, cockpit affordance, WorkItem class, standard, or generated artifact is not operationally adopted merely because code, docs, tests, or projections exist. Adoption requires an entry-consumption proof: `kernel.py --entry` or `kernel.py --context-pack` can route a representative cold-start task to the cheapest sufficient compressed band and can expose the stale, ambiguous, missing, unsafe, or provider-boundary failure route.
applies_when: a capability is intended to change how Type A agents navigate, choose context, apply work, or avoid broad search
a standard or skill says agents should use a new surface
a generated projection or evaluator is intended to prevent rediscovery
a provider lane, row-patch lane, or cockpit view should be reachable from ordinary operator phrasing
a WorkItem satisfaction claim depends on future agents discovering and consuming a new affordance

required_proofs.entry_route_proof: `./repo-python kernel.py --entry "<representative task>" --context-budget 12000` or `--context-pack` selects the capability, owning row, or typed failure packet without exact private prior knowledge.
required_proofs.compression_band_proof: The packet supplies the cheapest sufficient representation first: option row, card, capsule, projection, or graph hint before source body; exact source spans appear only for mutation or proof.
required_proofs.failure_route_proof: The packet says what to do when the target is stale, ambiguous, missing, unsafe, blocked by provider authority, or outside the generated projection.
required_proofs.dogfood_usage_proof: At least one realistic Type A task demonstrates that the agent used the entry-provided row or failure route, not merely that the row was present.
required_proofs.workitem_binding: The owning cap/WorkItem records satisfaction refs, execution receipt, or deferred residuals for any failed proof.

non_satisfaction: the test passes when invoked by exact filename
a standard mentions the surface but no entry packet routes to it
an exact `--ids` lookup works only after the agent already knows the stable id
a provider can generate data but no proof gate or row-patch route is exposed

python_exemplar.lesson: Python file/scope target resolution is adopted only when entry/context-pack emit file cards, symbol capsules, and stale-index/provider-boundary escalation routes that Type A tasks actually use.

entry_adoption_contract.audit_direction: Future audits should sample capability rows and ask whether a representative cold-start task reaches the row or typed failure route through --entry or --context-pack; exact-id success alone is insufficient.

observed_failure_capture_entry_contract.purpose: Ensure actor entry surfaces expose the Task Ledger capture reflex early enough that observed failures do not disappear into final prose.
observed_failure_capture_entry_contract.rule: First-read actor seeds and adapters must tell agents that an observed failing test, command error, failed validation, substrate drift, contradiction, or self-error has three legal closeout states: fix and validate it, create a Task Ledger WorkItem/capture, or cite an existing capture that names the original failure.
observed_failure_capture_entry_contract.repair_path: Add or refresh a compact entry-surface row that points to Task Ledger quick-capture rather than copying the full doctrine body, then run context-pack or entry-surface diagnostics to verify coverage.

partial_instruction_residual_capture_ent.purpose: Ensure broad operator prompts do not leave unexecuted deliverables stranded in native TODOs or final prose when the runtime completes only a slice.
partial_instruction_residual_capture_ent.rule: Agent runtime entry lenses and prompt hooks may emit a compact residual-capture nudge when a task has broad/deliverable/partial-execution pressure. The nudge must route to Task Ledger authority, not copy the Task Ledger doctrine body.
partial_instruction_residual_capture_ent.anti_bloat_rule: Entry surfaces carry only a trigger, owner route, and legal closeout states. Residual classification, ranking, shaping, and promotion stay in Task Ledger organizer/report surfaces.

capture_only_action_routing_boundary_con.purpose: Make todo/deliverable/capture prompts capture-only on the action side while preserving the navigation needed to file a well-routed Task Ledger capture.
capture_only_action_routing_boundary_con.rule: When the operator asks only to add a todo, add deliverables to todos, capture this, or equivalent capture-only wording, the legal substrate action is Task Ledger capture. Route discovery is encouraged when it improves the capture; implementing the captured work is banned.
routing_allowed_for_capture_quality: selected card, docs-route, or owner drilldowns needed to identify source refs, tags, suspected owner, and acceptance contour
forbidden_action_classes: editing substrate to address the captured todo's named deliverable
shaping, ranking, claiming, promoting, or executing the captured row
authoring paper modules, doctrine, skills, standards, frontend, docs, generated state, or release artifacts for the captured todo
using provider-native TODOs as durable backlog authority
capture_quality_fields: tags that name subsystem and failure class
suspected owner surface when discoverable by cheap routing
brief satisfaction or acceptance contour when known
non_satisfaction: banning all navigation drilldowns, which produces poorly routed captures
using capture wording as permission to implement the named work
leaving only adapter prose without a standard-owned contract
capture_only_action_routing_boundary_con.adapter_projection_rule: Adapter entry surfaces may summarize this as: capture-only means no implementation or promotion; route lookups are allowed when they make the capture accurate.

cross_authority_affinity_route_contract.purpose: Govern first-contact routes for read-only lenses that cluster one authority plane by pressure from another without merging their mutation authority.
cross_authority_affinity_route_contract.rule: When a projection lets one artifact family browse or cluster another, entry surfaces must name both authorities, identify the lens as read-only, expose the mutation lane for each authority, and preserve the anti-merge boundary.

mechanism_workitem_instance.source_authority: Mechanisms and mechanism standards own reusable how-patterns, doctrine/code behavior, and pressure labels.
mechanism_workitem_instance.clustered_authority: Task Ledger WorkItems own execution rows, event history, dispositions, and commitment state.
mechanism_workitem_instance.lens: Task Ledger cluster_flag ids of the form `mechanism:<mech_id>` expose WorkItems whose evidence or pressure is affiliated with a mechanism.
mechanism_workitem_instance.anti_merge_rule: Do not merge mechanisms and WorkItems into one artifact class. Use mechanism affinity clusters to find, shape, note, retire, or bind WorkItems through Task Ledger events; change mechanism doctrine only through mechanism/governing-standard lanes.

required_entry_fields: operator phrases that imply cross-authority confusion or affinity clustering
route to the source authority option surface
route to the clustered authority option surface
canonical skill or standard that states the anti-merge boundary
freshness or validation command for the projected lens
non_satisfaction: a mechanism card mentions WorkItems but no Task Ledger cluster route is exposed
a Task Ledger cluster exists but entry routing implies mechanisms are backlog rows
the route requires the agent to already know the exact mechanism id without an option-surface discovery step
the projection can be browsed but no owner mutation lane is named for either side

meta_navigation_first_contact_contract.purpose: Distinguish three first-contact lanes so the atlas/option-surface plane is route authority when the task target IS the navigation substrate, not a normal work item. Closes the silent failure where the entry packet's keyword-overlap classifier routes a meta-navigation task to an unrelated WorkItem.
meta_navigation_first_contact_contract.rationale: pri_128 says cold-agent entry surfaces must route to live compressed projections before bespoke triggers. The default cold-start contract names --entry as CONTROL_ENTRY and demotes ATLAS_PROJECTION to a drilldown. That ordering is correct when the task is normal work the entry packet can route to a relevant WorkItem, paper module, or skill. It silently fails when the task target IS the navigation contract itself: the entry packet then resolves the task by prose-keyword overlap (for example recognized_situation=imperative_authoring -> top-ranked WorkItem) and routes the agent away from the surface they were sent to repair. This contract names the third lane explicitly so first-contact for meta-navigation tasks does not depend on the entry packet's prose-keyword classifier succeeding.

lanes.applies_when: Task target is a normal repository work item (feature, bug, refactor, doctrine read, integration). The active phase or task ledger has a relevant WorkItem the entry packet can route to.
lanes.compliance: Run --entry first; follow its selected_lane and next_action.

lanes.applies_when: Task target is the navigation substrate itself (atlas, option-surface, kind contracts, route authority, first-contact behavior, navigation hologram theory) AND the entry packet's selected_lane misroutes (top WorkItem unrelated to navigation contract; suppressed candidate axioms relevant to the mission; banned routes include the very kind being repaired).
lanes.compliance: Run --entry once and persist its output as evidence of current classifier behavior. Do NOT follow its selected_lane.next_action when the routed WorkItem has no source_refs in navigation_substrate_target_set. Proceed to --kind-atlas / --option-surface as the lawful next move; the atlas is the route authority for this lane.

lanes.applies_when: Operator instruction explicitly says 'browse the atlas', 'use kind atlas', 'option-surface first', 'do not optimize keyword matching', or any phrase that names the atlas/option-surface plane as the first-move authority.
lanes.compliance: The operator's explicit browse instruction is sufficient to satisfy 'allowed_callers: explicit_operator_browse' on every option-surface. --entry is a courtesy receipt, not a gate.

navigation_substrate_target_set.rule: A task targets the navigation substrate when one or more of its named outcomes mutates the contracts/standards/paper modules/atlas kinds below. This is a substrate-derived list (stable ids), not a keyword list; the test is 'does the task ship change to one of these', not 'does the task wording mention navigation'.

entry_packet_misroute_signals.purpose: Shape a Type A agent can detect deterministically (from the entry packet output) when lane 'entry_compliance_receipt_only' applies. Structural matching against the entry packet itself, not prose keyword search.
signals: entry_packet.banned_routes contains a route_id whose surface is a substrate the operator's task targets (e.g. 'atlas_as_control_entry' is banned but the task is to repair the atlas contract).
entry_packet.selected_lane.reason routes to a top-ranked WorkItem whose source_refs / statement_snippet do not intersect with the task's navigation_substrate_target_set entries.

entry_packet.candidate_runtime_pressure.suppressed_rows contains rows whose match_overlap aligns with operator-cited candidate axioms (e.g. integration-not-greenfield, standards-are-shared-grammar) but were suppressed under 'first_contact_suppresses_query_only_token_overlap'.
entry_packet.recognized_situation classifies the task as 'imperative_authoring' but the recognized WorkItem cap_id has no source_refs in navigation_substrate_target_set.

compliance_receipt_semantics.rule: A Type A agent operating in lane 'entry_compliance_receipt_only' satisfies pri_128 and the canonical first-move contract by emitting an --entry call and persisting the output as evidence. The agent then proceeds to --kind-atlas / --option-surface as the route authority. The downstream WorkItem cited by the entry packet is not honored when its source_refs do not intersect navigation_substrate_target_set.
receipt_artifact_must_carry: candidate_routes that include the navigation substrate kinds the task targets
evidence_refs naming the misrouted entry packet's recognized_situation, selected_lane reason, and the unrelated top WorkItem cap_id
compliance_receipt_semantics.non_violation: Following lane 2 is NOT a violation of pri_128, atlas_as_control_entry, or 'agent_first_contact' bans. The contract recognizes that ATLAS_PROJECTION first-contact is the correct compliance posture when the task IS the atlas substrate and the entry packet structurally misroutes.

acceptance_checks.check: When the operator's task targets std_agent_entry_surface.json (this contract), the entry packet may classify the task as 'imperative_authoring' and route to the top WorkItem. This is a behavior-under-test signal, not authoritative routing. Compliance is satisfied by emitting one --entry receipt and proceeding to the atlas.
acceptance_checks.violation: Type A treats the entry packet's recognized_situation/next_action as binding when the routed WorkItem has no overlap with navigation_substrate_target_set, and authors content unrelated to the navigation contract.

acceptance_checks.check: Each lane's first_move command is callable from a cold session without depending on prose-keyword classifier success. Lane 1 routes through entry packet's WorkItem selection; lane 2 records entry packet output then opens kind_atlas; lane 3 opens kind_atlas directly under explicit operator browse authority.
acceptance_checks.violation: The kernel rejects --kind-atlas as first-contact when an operator explicitly instructed atlas browsing or when the task target is in navigation_substrate_target_set.

acceptance_checks.check: An agent_observations row exists and is reachable via './repo-python kernel.py --option-surface agent_observations --band flag', demonstrating the kind has a working write convention and at least one routed observation row.
acceptance_checks.violation: agent_observations row_count remains 0 after this contract lands; the kind is registered as a route in std_kind_atlas but has no demonstrated rows.

non_goals: Add a keyword classifier to the entry packet that detects meta-navigation tasks. This contract documents permission for Type A to operate when the existing classifier mismatches; it is not a new classifier.

Change the default coverage_ladder ordering in canonical_option_surface_routes.first_move_contract. Lane 1 (entry_mandatory_normal_task) remains the default. Lanes 2 and 3 are exception lanes triggered by structural conditions, not keyword overlap.

Promote ATLAS_PROJECTION to CONTROL_ENTRY at the surface_role level. The atlas remains an ATLAS_PROJECTION; this contract recognizes a class of tasks where ATLAS_PROJECTION first-contact is the correct compliance posture under explicit conditions.

entry_packet_misroute.task: make Type A cold-start atlas-first not prose-keyword-first

originating_evidence.operator_directive: browse the actual option surfaces: Kind Atlas -> relevant artifact kind cluster -> row cards/tapes -> governing standard/source authority

runtime_route_projection_contract.purpose: Prevent stable navigation behavior from living only in runtime classifier code.
runtime_route_projection_contract.rule: When a kernel route, classifier, docs-route, or entry packet recognizes a new stable lane, the same stable id, command template, owning artifact, freshness command, minimum read set, and actor_delivery decision must be present in the compressed projection substrate before the change is complete.
source_of_truth_order: codex/doctrine/skills/skill_registry.json when a skill family owns the lane
runtime classifier code consumes the projected rows
required_projection_surfaces: CODEX.md / CLAUDE.md adapter live blocks when shared actor delivery applies
codex/doctrine/routing_hologram.json when a skill route is the compressed row
docs/documentation_plane_map.md or docs/agent_instruction_router.md when documentation routing is the discovery plane
runtime_route_projection_contract.runtime_classifier_rule: Runtime matching may combine projected route tokens, docs-route tokens, and skill-registry/card anchors; it must not own a private phrase list that is sufficient to recognize the lane when the compressed route row is absent. Projected terms must carry match strength: explicit route tokens and skill trigger phrases may classify a lane, while incidental path words, standards vocabulary, broad compression terms, broad actor-axis labels such as Type A/Type B, or single generic words must not classify the lane by themselves.
acceptance_checks: Builder refresh completed for every generated surface touched.
Projection checker passes for agent_bootstrap and routing hologram drift.
A regression test proves the classifier consumes projected route terms.
A regression test or actor-delivery negative smoke proves a nearby generic navigation/projection task does not resolve to the specialized lane.
A regression test proves the live compressed route row exists.
Cold docs-route and --entry smokes resolve the same stable lane.
runtime_route_projection_contract.anti_pattern: Classifier-only navigation fix: --entry returns the new lane for tested strings, but cold agents reading AGENTS/bootstrap/docs-route cannot see the stable route.

typed_route_target_contract.purpose: Make situation routes point at typed owner surfaces instead of relying on prose labels, command strings, or private classifier phrase lists.
typed_route_target_contract.rule: New or materially refined situation routes should carry typed route targets naming the owner surfaces they route to. Each route target must name kind/ref plus role/relation/label, and a cold-start route target graph must include both an authority/explanation edge (governs, explains, evidences) and an operational/proof edge (implements, routes_to, validates, audits, projects). Targets whose relation is routes_to, validates, or audits are contact/proof edges and must carry a command so the next agent knows the authorized substrate contact. Runtime classifiers may consume route target refs, labels, roles, relations, and match_tokens as projected match terms, but must not treat generic target kind words as enough to classify a lane.
typed_route_target_contract.concept_route_rule: When a route's meaning depends on a doctrine concept or mechanism, name that doctrine node as a route_target and include the source artifact in the minimum read set if a cold actor needs the concept to act. A concept target explains what the route means; it does not replace the standard, skill, runtime surface, or builder that owns mutation.
typed_route_target_contract.target_ordering_rule: Prefer owner standard first, concept or mechanism anchor second, then operational skill/runtime/generated surfaces. The first target is the compact markdown chip; the full live sidecar remains the complete route graph.
typed_route_target_contract.projection_rule: agent_bootstrap_projection.normalize_situation_routes must preserve route_targets into agent_bootstrap_live.json and render compact target chips in generated entry markdown when a route is shown. Compact chips must include the target relation and, when available, one operational/contact target in addition to the first authority target. Generated rows remain projections; target refs remain the authority handles.
typed_route_target_contract.mutation_rule: If a route target names a standard, concept, mechanism, paper module, skill, principle, axiom candidate, or generated projection, the actual mutation belongs to that target's governing standard, doctrine standard, or builder lane. The situation route only routes attention and permission boundaries.
typed_route_target_contract.legacy_policy: Existing routes without route_targets remain valid during population. A route is population-debt, not invalid, until a task materially changes it or a classifier consumes it as a stable lane.
acceptance_checks: A refined route row in codex/doctrine/agent_bootstrap.json has route_targets with kind/ref.
A delivered cold-start route has target descriptors with role/relation/label and relation coverage for both why/authority and where/proof.
A delivered cold-start route has command-bearing contact targets for every routes_to, validates, or audits relation.
A concept-backed route includes a concept or mechanism route_target plus the governing standard or runtime owner that can mutate the route.
build_agent_bootstrap_projection.py preserves the targets in codex/doctrine/agent_bootstrap_live.json.
Generated entry markdown for a rendered route includes relation-bearing compact target chips and one operational/contact target when present.
Runtime projected match-term loading can use route target refs/match_tokens without adding a private phrase list.
typed_route_target_contract.anti_pattern: A stable route says 'open the relevant surface' or relies on a command string, but does not name the standard, paper module, skill, principle, generated projection, or runtime owner that explains what the route means.

navigation_index_spine_contract.purpose: Expose the generated unified artifact-kind index to entry, context-pack, and autonomous-seed continuity packets without making Atlas a first-contact control route.
entry_policy: System Atlas and Kind Atlas rows are labeled drilldowns after entry/context selection or explicit operator browse.
Coverage and materialization counts are diagnostics, not permission and not source authority.
Surface-coverage closure must expose whether behavior/watch rows require --coverage-enforcement-matrix before calling coverage fully closed.
Coverage-watch closure must carry a compact sequence that names the matrix command, watch-row selector, owner repair fields, and rerun success check.

Autonomous recursive seed consumers should receive a bounded coverage_watch_snapshot when the matrix is available, including current watch kind ids, compact row-owned repair classes, and the same rerun closure check.

type_b_to_type_a_handoff_framing_contrac.purpose: Route Type A/B claims through the canonical actor-axis concept before interpreting a handoff, and prevent operator-pasted Type B continuation responses from being mistaken for instructions for some other future actor.
type_b_to_type_a_handoff_framing_contrac.preclassification_rule: Before acting on labels such as Type A, Type B, bridge, provider, subagent, operator HUD, worker, or delegated, classify the actor by substrate_type, class_tier, surface, delegation_role, authority, context_mode, lane, and thinking_budget. Do not infer Type B from delegation or worker language alone.
type_b_to_type_a_handoff_framing_contrac.rule: When an operator asks the current repo agent to ask Type B, the receiving Type A agent must first resolve the actor axes, then normally treat Type B as an operator-mediated external cognition lane: prepare a bounded paste-ready ask for chatgpt_web/operator_chrome_hud/bridge, not a native Agent-tool spawn. When an operator later pastes a Type B/bridge response containing a Type A continuation prompt, the receiving repo agent must identify itself as Type A, treat the Type B text as advisory synthesis handed over by the operator, and execute the embedded Type A continuation prompt after verifying live repo state. The agent must not answer as if it is not Type A, must not ask who Type A is, must not spawn a tool-using subagent merely because the prompt says Type B, and must not classify the paste from broad Type A/Type B labels alone.

operator_mediated_type_b_rule.type_b_from_type_a_perspective: operator-carried external/HUD reasoning over materialized context; no private filesystem, command, edit, test, or commit authority
operator_mediated_type_b_rule.normal_type_a_action: explore live substrate, produce a paste-ready Type B ask with evidence and ASK_TYPE_A return requirements, then wait for or consume the operator-pasted response
operator_mediated_type_b_rule.forbidden_confusion: Do not call native subagent tools as the Type B lane. Tool-using Codex/Claude subagents are delegated Type A substrate actors.

shuttle_trace_context_contract.purpose: Make operator-mediated Type B handoffs behave like a traceable A/B shuttle: Type B supplies bounded synthesis plus exact inward-look requests, and Type A verifies live substrate state before mutating and returns evidence that improves the next Type B pass.
applies_when: A Prompt Shelf carryforward, compact, continue, instantiate, or Type B primer prompt crosses the operator-mediated Type B to Type A boundary.
A pasted packet, clip, screenshot, source chunk, or Type B response asks the repo agent to continue live substrate work.
A tab/session/work-item/packet lineage question could otherwise collapse volatile viewport labels into durable identity.
required_trace_fields: volatile viewport aliases, labeled as aliases
provider thread or session ids when available
source artifacts such as clip ids, source_text_hashes, prompt_run_ids, commits, CAPs, and receipts
authority-backed work objects, with title-inferred hints labeled as hints
landed, proposed, stale, and hypothesis state
return_evidence_needed for the next Type B pass
type_b_obligations: Do not claim private substrate facts.
Infer the strongest useful abstraction from the supplied packet and evidence.
Name exact Type A inward-look targets and disconfirming checks.
Preserve durable artifact identity without treating tab ordinals, titles, or visible DOM as authority.

For B2.2 semantic carryforward, do not let terminal-state evidence become the deliverable; a terminal-state recap is never sufficient without a change-bearing next-wave primitive or explicit no-mutation disconfirming evidence.

When recommending verification, no-op, or already-landed posture, name the non-null exit candidates: owner lane to close, residual to retire or update, route/projection mechanism to improve, or explicit no-edit receipt fields Type A must prove.

semantic_carryforward_admissibility.purpose: Prevent B2.2 from obeying source/evidence honesty while failing the continuation-selection job.
applies_when: A B2.2 semantic carryforward response is built from dense trace, landed commit, validation, receipt, or closeout evidence.
The operator corrects, redirects, or expresses frustration that the prior response stopped at status instead of selecting the next governed primitive.
admissible_outputs: Type A patch packet with owner-surface candidates, disconfirming checks, and return evidence.
WorkItem or owner-surface update when the next governed primitive is residual/capture/routing rather than source code.
Explicit no-mutation receipt only when it names the disconfirming evidence, stewardship lane checked, next best lane checked, and reentry condition.

type_a_obligations: Do not blindly implement the latest Type B packet.
Inspect named inward-look targets first when the task is not trivially local.
Compare sibling or ancestor surfaces before greenfielding.
Report whether the result was reuse, finish-half-built, import-from-annex, or build-new.
Return receipts with files, commands, tests, stale projections, missing links, and Task Ledger or WorkItem bindings when relevant.

When live verification disproves the pasted blocker or finds no source patch is warranted, do not yield on already-exists alone; close the next owner lane, bind or retire a residual, patch the entry/projection mechanism, or return a no-edit receipt with stewardship_checked, next_best_lane_checked, and a reentry condition.

no_edit_pass_floor.purpose: Prevent Type A/Type B shuttle passes from consuming a full live-state cycle and returning only 'already true' or 'nothing to edit' when a bounded substrate-care lane remains.
no_edit_pass_floor.type_b_prompting_rule: Type B packets that conclude the target is likely landed must still provide Type A with a non-null closeout contour: what to retire, what to project, what owner lane to resume, or what no-edit receipt fields would make stopping valid.
no_edit_pass_floor.type_a_closeout_rule: Type A may stop without a patch only after proving the owner lane is closed or unsafe, the residual state is bound, and the next best bounded improvement lane has been checked.
no_edit_pass_floor.resolved_blocker_acceleration_rule: When live verification proves the requested blocker is already resolved, that fact becomes a launch condition rather than a closeout condition: Type A must immediately consume it into the next downstream substrate action, such as satisfaction refs, residual closure, owner-lane update, routing affordance repair, or the next bounded feature specimen. The pass may stop at verified-already-resolved only when mutation is unsafe and a durable blocker or intake item records the reentry condition.

authority_boundaries: Tab ordinals are display-only viewport aliases.
Provider thread, work object, and artifact lineage are durable identity candidates.
Title text is a hint, not authority.
Visible DOM is an observation, not complete history.
Compacted packets are lineage artifacts, not mere pasted text.
Dynamic live tab facts belong in generated projections such as an operator_shuttle_trace_context or Tab Situation Atlas packet, not static prompt prose.

role_specific_prompt_deltas.B3: Compact acts as trace compiler: preserve terminal state, proof boundary, source artifact identity, work object/CAP/commit linkage, stale or missing projections, Type B decision, Type A inward-look target, and return evidence.
role_specific_prompt_deltas.B2_2: Semantic carryforward produces the best abstraction plus exact Type A inward-look request, disconfirming check, and change-bearing next-wave primitive; terminal-state recap is never sufficient.
role_specific_prompt_deltas.B2: Continue carries trace context and performs the inward look before patching when prior Type B named targets.
role_specific_prompt_deltas.B2_1: Continue concise stays concise without dropping shuttle handle, artifact ids, work object, terminal state, or next inward-look target.
role_specific_prompt_deltas.B1: Instantiate establishes initial episode identity, known aliases, candidate work object, provider/session id when available, owner surfaces, and unknowns.
role_specific_prompt_deltas.type_b_primer: Primer emphasizes narrow-packet reasoning, private-state boundary, and interview-Type-A posture.

prompt_family_telos_contract.purpose: Make the Prompt Shelf family preserve the operator's actual intent, work-episode telos, evidence boundary, and next useful action without flattening every prompt into the long B2.2 semantic-carryforward shape.
applies_when: A Prompt Shelf prompt starts, continues, compacts, primes, or receives an operator-mediated Type A/B shuttle episode.
Recent captured Prompt Shelf outputs show role drift, generic continuation, overlong primer prose, or lost operator intent across a multi-tab thread.
A prompt-family refinement should improve response quality from observed runs rather than from prompt-body aesthetics alone.
shared_kernel: Preserve the operator's actual intent, not only literal wording.
Carry forward the work episode's telos: the outcome this thread is trying to make real.
Separate durable identity and evidence from volatile viewport context.
Prefer reuse, finish-half-built, import-from-annex, or dynamic packet/projection before greenfielding.
When evidence is missing, name the exact inward-look target instead of inventing private state.
When acting, choose the largest coherent reversible move inside the trust envelope.
When compacting, preserve lineage, terminal state, proof boundary, residuals, and next action only when source-stated.
When concise, compress words, not identity or intent.

role_specific_telos.B1: Instantiate births the work episode: infer telos, candidate owner surfaces, durable identity, unknowns, and first inward-look route without overbuilding.
role_specific_telos.B2: Continue moves the episode forward: integrate the newest delta into prior state, preserve telos, and choose the largest coherent reversible next action.
role_specific_telos.B2_1: Continue concise stays short without dropping telos, identity, current decision, one disconfirming check, or the next inward-look target.
role_specific_telos.B2_2: Semantic carryforward remains the phenotype donor: select the strongest semantic continuation, pass the teleology/composition-root/reversible-next-wave/evidence-boundary gates, and make the abstraction evaluable through Type A look-around.
role_specific_telos.B3: Compact compiles lineage: preserve source-stated cognitive artifacts, terminal state, proof boundary, residuals, and handoff affordances without inventing recommendations.
role_specific_telos.A2: Type A Continue executes the episode telos in the live substrate, verifies inward-look targets, and returns receipts that improve the next Type B pass.
role_specific_telos.type_b_primer: The Type B primer carries evergreen ethos as a dense constitutional microkernel while dynamic state stays in generated packets, facts projections, and ASK_TYPE_A handles.

prompt_family_telos_contract.primer_density_rule: Separate minimal shuttle trace identity from larger ethos/baggage: the primer should carry ranked evergreen decision rules plus dynamic-packet references, not repeated long prompt-contract prose or live facts baked into static text.

rosetta_stone_principle.naming_correction_note: Originally tagged pri_117_candidate during 2026-04-25 entry-surface reorganization session; collided with existing pri_117 (Paper Modules Must Be Navigable Through The Navigation Layer). Renamed to pri_121_candidate (next sequential after pri_120) on the same day. The underlying principle and raw-seed anchor (par_phase_09_raw_seed__naming_a_structural_drift_signal_is_not_the_same_as_routing_it_017) are unchanged.
rosetta_stone_principle.statement: When an artifact is over its compression budget, refine the standard governing it so the artifact carries less hand-authored content and more substrate-derived projection. The compressed surface becomes a Rosetta Stone — the minimum representation that lets a reader expand to the full meaning by following the route. The expansion is dynamic, derived from substrate, and fits the budget by construction.
voice_motifs_preserved: refine its own standard so that it has further compression
doesn't have to be a PaperMod, but it could be something else depending
rosetta_stone_principle.generalises_how: compressed_entry_projection said 'route pointers, not protocol bodies' for entry surfaces only; pri_117 extends to any artifact whose governing standard can be refined to enable deeper projection (paper module, skill, generated table, builder-rendered region, machine sidecar — kind dictates destination).
rosetta_stone_principle.operational_test: An over-budget artifact's standard declares: (a) what stays hand-authored, (b) what gets builder-projected from substrate, (c) the budget the surface must fit, (d) the freshness command. The artifact then either fits the budget via projection, or the standard tightens further until it does.

compression_via_projection_contract.purpose: Codify how an over-budget agent entry surface compresses by projection rather than by authoring.
compression_via_projection_contract.applies_when: byte size of any markdown surface in scope exceeds its configured budget OR forbidden_payload patterns surface in adapter prose

decision_table.use_when: The compressed content is a subsystem ontology, a what-it-IS explanation, or a fan-in target for multiple skills/standards.
decision_table.projection_path: adapter row -> trigger phrase + one-line rule + ./repo-python kernel.py --paper-module <slug> + freshness command

decision_table.use_when: The compressed content is a named move, a protocol, an iteration diagnostic, or a triggers/workflow pair.
decision_table.projection_path: adapter row -> trigger phrase + one-line rule + ./repo-python kernel.py --entry "<task>" --context-budget 12000 + stable skill row drilldown; ./repo-python kernel.py --skill-find <exact> --debug is debug trace only

decision_table.use_when: The compressed content is a rule, a schema, a validator, or a contract that mutates only through apply lanes.
decision_table.projection_path: adapter row -> trigger phrase + one-line rule + path to standard + freshness command
decision_table.owning_standard: self (the new standard) plus the standard-of-standards contract

decision_table.use_when: The content can be derived deterministically from substrate at build time (live facts, indexes, byte counts, drift status). Currently the canonical example is the live-context block + the paper-module index in adapter markdown, both rendered by build_agent_bootstrap_projection.py.
decision_table.projection_path: adapter <!-- BEGIN ... --> ... <!-- END ... --> region rendered by builder
decision_table.owning_standard: self (the projection contract); builder is the only writer of the region

decision_table.use_when: Content is too dense for human prose but needs structured machine consumption (e.g. agent_bootstrap_live.json, agent_bootstrap_injection_strip.json).
decision_table.projection_path: adapter pointer + sidecar path + consumer note

rosetta_stone_shape.expansion_contract: The route_command, when run, returns the full corpus the surface compressed away. The freshness_command, when run, returns a non-stale signal or a stale-list with repair guidance. Together they let the reader expand the Rosetta Stone seed to the full meaning without re-deriving it.
rosetta_stone_shape.anti_pattern: An adapter row that paraphrases or excerpts the routed artifact's content beyond the trigger + rule. Excerpting drifts; routing does not.

standard_refinement_protocol: Identify its governing standard (the one that declares what may live in it).
Add or refine the standard's compression rules: byte budget, allowed_payload, forbidden_payload, max_authored_section_count, projection_required_for: [list of section_kinds].
Author the projector if missing (builder, kernel command, generated region, sidecar).
Compress the artifact: replace forbidden-payload sections with Rosetta Stone seeds + projection routes.
Run the audit (post-extension; see std_agent_entrypoint_audit) to verify no violation remains.
Cite the principle: par_phase_09_raw_seed__...routing_it_017 (or pri_117 once minted).

common_sense_helpfulness_floor.purpose: Prevent entry-surface and navigation discipline from becoming anti-helpful ceremony.
common_sense_helpfulness_floor.rule: Typed routes, hooks, audits, and doctrine constrain bad navigation; they do not prohibit obvious bounded work. Agents should perform read-only, reversible, or tightly scoped checks when they materially advance the task, and should reserve permission gates for high-blast-radius or genuinely ambiguous decisions.

action_over_pointless_inaction.rule: When bounded, reversible, read-only, or clearly path-scoped work would reduce uncertainty or move the active thread forward, take the largest coherent verified action inside the trust envelope before writing more meta-analysis, handoff prose, or permission-gate language.
action_over_pointless_inaction.default_bias: The default failure mode is not acting too early; it is twiddling thumbs or shipping a token safe slice while re-describing a problem whose stronger coherent move is already available.
action_over_pointless_inaction.test: Before emitting another synthesis, ASK packet, or defer-to-next-session note, name the largest coherent useful wave available now. If it is safe and in-scope, do it; if you choose a smaller slice, state the blocker that made the larger wave unsafe or unavailable.

operator_command_execution_policy.rule: When a repo-owned command, apply lane, checkpoint lane, builder, test, status check, or operator-control route exists and the current actor has substrate access, the agent executes it or wires it into an operator-facing launcher/status surface. The operator should not be handed CLI commands as the required next step.
allowed_command_mentions: diagnostic evidence already run by the agent
receipt or reproducibility detail for another Type A agent
agent-facing documentation, tests, or automation configuration
blocked evidence when execution was unsafe and the blocker is named
anti_patterns: Telling the operator to run a shell command that the current Type A actor can safely run.
Using a command block as a substitute for executing an available reversible repo-owned action.
Closing with command homework instead of a receipt when the command was in scope.
operator_command_execution_policy.good_pattern: I ran the repo-owned lane, verified the receipt, and report the SHA/status; if unsafe, I recorded the blocker and exact owner surface.

kernel_reliance_calibration.rule: Use kernel for authority, freshness, route selection, active runtime state, high-blast-radius gating, and bounded context assembly. Do not use kernel as a substitute for direct substrate checks.
kernel_reliance_calibration.bypass_rule: After the minimum entry packet identifies the relevant surface, if the next uncertainty is a known-path, reversible, read-only, test, diff, grep, import, help, or scoped-edit question, use direct local tools first and report the result.
kernel_reliance_calibration.second_kernel_call_test: Before a second kernel call in the same mission phase, decide whether the next question is kernel-shaped. If it is not about authority, freshness, route selection, active state, blast radius, or bounded cross-kind context, bypass kernel.

non_null_pass_yield.rule: A substantive pass is not complete merely because the requested surface already exists. If discovery finds reusable learning, a route gap, a blocked write, an unused affordance, or residual work, the pass must leave a durable yield through the best available transport or emit a deferred capture packet.
non_null_pass_yield.test: A final closeout that says nothing_to_refine is valid only when it also shows no needed patch, no residual, no route gap, no blocked write, no reusable lesson, and an explicit stewardship or next-best-lane check.
non_null_pass_yield.no_repeated_null_pass_rule: A Type A pass may not return a second consecutive null, no-op, idle, CAP-only, verify-only, or settlement-only closeout on the same teleology without broadening to the next bounded substrate-care lane. If the requested lane is exhausted, the agent asks what the operator was actually trying to improve and acts on the next safe owner surface: propagation, validation ergonomics, projection hygiene, context safety, concurrency, resource pressure, route discoverability, generated-state correctness, hidden mutation audit, closeout truthfulness, or operator-friction repair. It stops only when those lanes are unsafe, claimed, policy-bound, or genuinely absent, and names the exact re-entry condition.
non_null_pass_yield.pro_forma_clearance_invalid_rule: The words stewardship_checked, next_best_lane_checked, and reentry_condition are not receipts by themselves. If next_best_lane_checked names a next useful lane and no exact unsafe/claimed/policy-bound/genuinely-absent rejection reason is recorded, Type A must act on that lane or capture the blocker before closeout. Field names alone are null-pass laundering.
non_null_pass_yield.resolved_blocker_acceleration_rule: A resolved blocker is not a pass terminal state. The closeout must bind the resolution to a downstream owner action in the same wave unless authority health, write contention, policy, or scope collision makes mutation unsafe; in that case the pass records a durable blocker/intake item with exact source refs and the first safe downstream action.
non_null_pass_yield.settlement_is_not_refinement_rule: Generated-state, Task Ledger, Work Ledger, or other bookkeeping settlement is not refinement. Settlement completion, validation success, or clean git status cannot justify refinement_result: nothing_to_refine until a stewardship lens checks source-bundle coverage, audit/sidecar/source pair coverage, unexpected projection tails, owner-tool contract gaps, repeated operator-friction patterns, and whether manual operator challenge was required to reveal the real patch.
nothing_to_refine_clearance: stewardship_checked: true with checked surfaces and residual-free evidence
next_best_lane_checked: true with lanes acted on, or lanes rejected and reasons
concrete WorkItem/CAP for a durable blocker
motivating_examples: Standing Latency Governor observed that no latency lane cleared threshold, then stopped or CAP'd even though the broader teleology was efficiency, concurrency, context safety, operator-friction reduction, and substrate care.

Generated-state settlement landed Task Ledger / Work Ledger projections and reported refinement_result: nothing_to_refine before the Task Ledger source bundle omission revealed an actual owner-tool refinement.
A verification pass proved the named pressure lane was already covered, then reported no new patch plus next_best_lane_checked without acting on or rejecting the next safe substrate-care lane.

commit_autonomy_policy.rule: Inside the trust envelope, agents make ordinary local commit judgment themselves. When a task produces durable repo changes, finish with a scoped commit unless the operator explicitly says not to commit. A dirty tree is not a reason to skip commit: identify this run's owned paths, stage only bounded owned pathspecs, leave unrelated pre-existing dirt untouched, and report what was excluded. If owned changes are entangled with pre-existing dirty files, make the narrowest safe commit and record the entanglement; skip only when path attribution is genuinely impossible or the change is unsafe/invalid.

standing_private_internal_authorization.rule: The default answer is yes for safe scoped private/local/internal repo work: edits, tests, projection rebuilds, local app reinstall/relaunch, internal receipts, Task/Work Ledger closeout, and scoped commits should proceed without re-asking merely for approval.
standing_private_internal_authorization.public_release_boundary: Public push, public deploy, publication, external dissemination, social posting, public demo upload, recipient send, or other externally visible release action is not authorized by this standing grant and requires a fresh explicit public-release instruction.
standing_private_internal_authorization.projection_rule: When a gate has consumed the standing private/internal grant, projections should report internal_authorization=satisfied and public_release_authorization=not_authorized_by_operator rather than generic pending operator authorization.
limits: No destructive, irreversible, secret, or disclosure boundary without task-specific authority.
No bypass of Work Ledger claims, scoped path ownership, validation, generated-region ownership, or dirty-tree isolation.
No public release action unless the operator separately authorizes public release.

commit_when: changed paths are owned by the current task or can be pathspec-isolated even while unrelated dirty files exist
source authority, generated projection, and runtime sidecar changes have been classified
generated files were produced by their owning builder rather than hand-edited
meaningful validation ran, or the validation gap is explicitly documented
unrelated dirty paths are excluded from the scoped commit and named in the closeout
no secret, disclosure, destructive, irreversible, or publication boundary is crossed
no live same-path concurrent owner conflict is present
commit_autonomy_policy.same_path_conflict_freshness_rule: A same-path conflict is only a blocker when it is live at closeout time. Before citing pre-existing or session-start dirt as non-owned, re-run a pathspec status check for the alleged blocker paths and refresh the Work Ledger claim view or mutation check. If the pathspec is clean or the owning session has already landed/finalized, retire the stale blocker and proceed with the current owned scoped commit or generated-state settlement; do not ask the operator to choose a checkpoint lane solely from an initial dirty snapshot.
escalate_only_for: push, pull request, remote sync, or other publication
non-isolatable owned dirty surfaces after a real path-attribution attempt
failing validation that changes patch safety
explicit operator instruction not to commit
commit_autonomy_policy.safe_transport: Use explicit pathspecs or tools/meta/control/scoped_commit.py for scoped commits; split unrelated concerns into separate commits; report hash, validation, excluded paths, and any same-path entanglement. Do not broaden to checkpoint/save-everything/commit-everything/clean-branch unless the operator explicitly asked for the broad lane or path attribution is genuinely impossible. In the private-repo trust envelope, those broad-save words select the checkpoint lane after quick safety checks rather than per-path ownership ceremony.
commit_autonomy_policy.anti_permission_gate: Do not end routine work with say the word, confirm, your call, or ready for me to commit. Do not convert stale session-start dirt into an operator-choice gate after live path status proves the blocker path is clean.

adjacent_capability_lane.rule: Agents may surface, propose, and when safely bounded sometimes execute genuinely high-value adjacent capability improvements discovered during work. This lane exists so useful outside-the-box capability gains are not suppressed merely because they were not the literal original ask. It is not blanket permission for arbitrary initiative; the bar is high, the check is principled, the scope is bounded, and the operator-visible receipt is mandatory.
gates: The idea is genuinely capability-increasing, not decorative, self-indulgent, or merely clever.
The agent can name the concrete future capability unlocked.
The idea passes applicable axioms, principles, standards, safety rules, and blast-radius checks.
The action is reversible, read-only, tightly path-scoped, or otherwise inside the trust envelope; otherwise capture or propose instead of executing.
The agent surfaces the deviation to the operator with an honest receipt that names the work as adjacent.
The original task state remains honest; adjacent work must not be laundered as completion of the original ask.
The agent rejects mediocre, speculative, or self-indulgent ideas even when they are interesting.
adjacent_capability_lane.slogan: Do not suppress good adjacent capability ideas. Do not launder mediocre ideas as outside-the-box.

canonical_example.summary: CLAUDE.md Compact Instructions section was adjacent to the original B3 prompt-shelf compaction ask, but useful enough to keep because it filled an empty Claude Code auto-compaction prior surface, was low-blast-radius, reversible, scoped, committed, pushed, and receipt-captured while the original B3 routing miss was separately acknowledged in its own cap rather than smuggled under the adjacent ship.

anti_patterns: Using adjacent capability work to avoid or obscure the original task.
Calling a mediocre or speculative idea a capability gain because it feels creative or 'outside the box'.
Crossing irreversible, destructive, disclosure, publication, or high-blast-radius boundaries without operator decision under the cover of serendipity.
Adding doctrine, schemas, or surfaces before checking whether an existing owner already covers the behavior.
Conflating adjacent ship with completion of the original ask in the receipt or summary.
adjacent_capability_lane.test: Before shipping an adjacent capability improvement, name the concrete future capability unlocked, confirm the action is reversible or tightly bounded, confirm no axiom/principle/safety boundary is crossed, and confirm the original task state remains honest in a separate receipt. If any of those checks fails, capture or propose instead of executing.

anti_patterns: Treating a hook warning against one bad shell pattern as a ban on all shell use.
Stopping at a plausible hypothesis when the next disambiguating check is cheap and bounded.
Using ASK_TYPE_A for routine read-only checks.
Restarting or reframing the whole task when the operator adds a side instruction.
Hand-authoring bulky adapter prose instead of projecting compact route pointers from substrate.
Reading the session-start git status header as live truth without re-running git status when claims rest on it.
Re-describing the problem when an obvious bounded check or scoped patch is available.
Using propagation work as a substitute for the technical repair it was supposed to enable.
Asking for routine commit/checkpoint confirmation after a coherent reversible path-scoped slice is validated.
Using concurrency checks as a new permission gate when explicit pathspec commits or scoped_commit.py can isolate the slice.
Ending with 'next session should...' when the current actor can safely run the next read-only check, local validation, or coupled reversible patch now.
Treating perfect framing as higher priority than useful action.
Treating boundedness as an ambition cap instead of a blast-radius and proof-discipline envelope.
Replacing a broad system-level operator complaint with one token proof case or audit when prompt, doctrine, standard, runtime, and test surfaces are coupled and safe to move together.
Treating Type B bridge work as private-substrate synthesis only, or refusing online research because exact private repo state is unavailable.
Ending a pass with 'already exists' or 'nothing_to_refine' after finding a blocked write, route gap, unused affordance, or reusable lesson.
Treating settlement, validation, clean git status, or bookkeeping completion as proof that refinement is absent.
Returning a repeated null/no-op/idle/CAP-only closeout on the same teleology without broadening to the next bounded substrate-care lane.

Stopping a latency or governor pass at 'no clean high-value lane' while adjacent efficiency, concurrency, context-safety, projection-hygiene, or operator-friction lanes are safe and owner-addressable.
Calling kernel again for a known-path local question when rg, cat, sed, git diff/status, py_compile, pytest, or a direct help command would answer it inside the trust envelope.
positive_patterns: Continue the active thread while incorporating new operator constraints additively.
Run obvious bounded diagnostics before asking for more context.
Separate parked high-blast-radius work from safe local preflight.
Use typed surfaces where they exist, and use exact local commands where they are the clearest bounded check.
When a hook corrects one shell pattern, learn the specific anti-pattern (do not pipe past structured payloads) without inferring a global ban.
Prefer the largest coherent useful action now over a better-framed inaction later.
After a doctrine or prompt propagation patch, return to the active technical thread and advance at least the next bounded check.
Convert 'we should inspect X' into running the inspection when the actor has repo access and the command is read-only.
Use handoff prose to preserve state after action, not instead of action.
Commit validated path-scoped local slices without re-asking; local commit is memory, not publication.
Use explicit pathspecs or scoped_commit.py in dirty shared trees and report excluded paths.
When the operator asks for an ambitious system correction, move every coupled reversible surface you can verify in the same turn and name only the genuinely blocked remainder.

For bridge prompts, split lanes explicitly: request Type A evidence for decisive private facts and use web/search tools for public prior art, current docs, repos, specs, papers, and disconfirming evidence.
When exact mutation transport is blocked by an exclusive claim, emit a deferred capture packet or record an alternate durable receipt instead of collapsing to no-op.

After entry identifies the surface or path, use direct local reads, search, diffs, tests, profilers, and scoped edits for obvious reversible work; return to kernel when the uncertainty becomes authority, freshness, route, runtime-state, blast-radius, or bounded-context shaped.

allowed_payload: dynamic live facts from the bootstrap builder

forbidden_payload: actor-specific operating deltas that belong in CODEX.md or CLAUDE.md
raw source duplication from paper modules or skills
full shared subsystem docs already reachable from AGENTS.md

allowed_payload: Claude-specific constraints, hooks, and follow-on carriers
forbidden_payload: full shared subsystem docs already reachable from AGENTS.md

entry_affordance_contract.rule: An entry affordance must be enough to route, not enough to replace the routed artifact.

candidate_runtime_pressure_contract.purpose: Allow failure-explanatory axiom candidates to influence Type A operating packets without treating candidates as active doctrine.
candidate_runtime_pressure_contract.non_goal: Do not promote, bulk-promote, or treat candidate axioms as settled law inside entry/context packets. Do not hand-mint pri_* from this surfacing path.

match_priority_order.trigger: task wording or selected-WorkItem evidence contains the literal axiom id substring (e.g. axiom_candidate_<slug_underscored>)
task wording or selected-WorkItem evidence contains the literal slug substring (length >= 4)

token overlap (>= 2 content tokens, length >= 4, stopword-stripped) between selected-WorkItem evidence_texts and candidate (title, slug, formal_clause, flag/card band, activation surface_when/packet_clause)
token overlap (>= 2 content tokens, length >= 4, stopword-stripped) between task query and candidate (title, slug, formal_clause, flag/card band, activation surface_when/packet_clause)

candidate_runtime_pressure_contract.first_contact_filter_policy: First-contact entry/context packets may compute deterministic_token_overlap as an explainable suppression receipt, but MUST NOT emit those rows in candidate_runtime_pressure.rows unless the row also resolves through explicit candidate id, explicit slug, selected-WorkItem evidence, or a whitelisted operator autonomy pressure phrase. Query-only token overlap belongs in suppressed_rows, not operating guidance.
candidate_runtime_pressure_contract.evidence_texts_contract: Callers SHOULD populate evidence_texts from the selected-WorkItem payload (id, title, statement, statement_snippet, source_refs, dependencies, notes) so that WorkItem-cited candidates surface even when the bare task query would not match. Richer WorkItem payload (full statement, notes[], source_refs[], dependencies[]) is follow-on debt declared in std_system_axiom_candidate.json::curation_cadence_contract::follow_on_implementation_debt.
surface_when: the operator explicitly cites an axiom candidate by id or slug in the prompt or task wording
a selected WorkItem cites or depends on an axiom candidate via source_refs, dependencies, or notes
a selected doctrine artifact (paper module, standard, principle, mechanism, concept) cites an axiom candidate

the operator uses a whitelisted action-autonomy phrase such as permission-gated, largest coherent, autonomous seed, seed should work, or work for anything, and the matching axiom candidate is in the autonomy whitelist
candidate_runtime_pressure_contract.rule: When an axiom candidate is runtime-eligible AND the task pressure matches its surface_when, the entry/context packet MUST surface a candidate_runtime_pressure block under an explicit candidate label with a drilldown to the tape band. The packet MUST NOT present the clause as active doctrine, MUST NOT bind future actors, and MUST keep authority_posture=candidate_not_active_doctrine.
anti_patterns: Surfacing the candidate clause without the candidate label (looks like active doctrine).
Using fuzzy semantic match without a deterministic explainable token overlap (this wave).
Auto-promoting a candidate when it surfaces (promotion is operator/controller-governed).

agent_operating_packet_contract.purpose: Give Type A agents the smallest durable principle/axiom frame at entry without loading the raw principle corpus or treating candidate axioms as law.
source_authority_order: obsidian/okay lets do this/09 - Raw-Seed Preservation, Semantic Reset, and Fresh Execution Spine/raw_seed/raw_seed_principles.json
obsidian/okay lets do this/09 - Raw-Seed Preservation, Semantic Reset, and Fresh Execution Spine/raw_seed/system_axiom_candidates.json

classification_rules.global_always: status == active and scope_profile.scope_id == global.constitutional_doctrine; project tiny+flag into Type A entry strips.
classification_rules.global_frequent: status == active and scope_profile.domain == global, excluding global.constitutional_doctrine; route by task situation, do not always inject.
classification_rules.agent_principles: status == active and scope_profile.scope_id in configured agent_principle_scope_ids; same source row shape as principles, but runtime_doctrine_type=agent_principle and entry strips carry ids/counts only.
classification_rules.situational: all other active principles; open through --entry, --context-pack, paper-module, or option-surface drilldowns.
classification_rules.axiom_candidates: candidate rows remain candidate_not_active_doctrine and may surface only as candidate pressure with non-law warning.

agent_operating_packet_contract.entry_surface_rule: Bootstrap strips and --context-pack include only the strip projection; --entry may add a tiny task-conditioned agent_principle_lens with selected ids, one-line flags, and invocation commands. Full principle statements, evidence, tests, and axiom tape remain behind explicit drilldown routes.
agent_operating_packet_contract.byte_accounting_rule: The packet must report compact full, pretty full, global capsule, and entry strip UTF-8 byte counts so runtime policy can choose between always-global, frequent, and situational projections.
anti_patterns: Injecting every active principle into first-contact context.
Collapsing global_frequent principles into global_always because they are broadly useful.
Presenting axiom candidates as axioms or active doctrine.
Copying raw-seed principle bodies into AGENTS.md, CODEX.md, CLAUDE.md, or AGENTS.override.md instead of routing to the packet.
Adding a broad always-on agent-principle prose block instead of a task-conditioned lens plus drilldown command.

actor_delivery_contract.rule: A durable navigation, comprehension, protocol, generated artifact, standard, skill, or checker is not complete merely because it exists and passes local tests. If it changes how agents should navigate or answers a recurring operator question class, record an explicit actor-delivery decision.
applies_when: a new route or artifact changes first-contact behavior
a new artifact answers a recurring operator question class
a new checker, standard, or skill changes what agents must trust, avoid, or verify
a compressed artifact is intended to prevent grep, tribal knowledge, or rediscovery
a process-audit or navigation-metabolism finding shows agents repeatedly miss an existing surface
decision_required_when: a durable surface changes first-contact behavior or recurring operator question routing
a route or artifact is intended to change how agents trust, avoid, verify, or discover system state
a compact generated artifact is intended to prevent rediscovery or grep-first behavior
decision_not_required_when: the surface is a transient runtime row that is not a durable situation_route
the route is a raw debug trace with no stable actor-facing id
the artifact is an implementation detail reachable only after a stable id has already been selected
the owner has not accepted the route/artifact into a durable catalog

decision_coverage.rule: Every durable situation_route must have an explicit delivery posture. Cold-start delivery is one rare, budget-gated posture; context_pack_only, drilldown_only, private_root_only, debug_only, and defer_with_workitem are valid when the boundary and reason are recorded.
decision_coverage.unknown_blocking_policy: Unknown decisions are actionable debt during migration; they become checker blockers only when a route is marked enforcement_required or the standard is explicitly raised to hard enforcement.

required: compact Rosetta in generated actor surfaces
fallback and governing-standard anchors in the compact Rosetta when the situation route defines them
actor receipt proves delivery for each required actor
fast actor receipt reports structural delivery without running latency-heavy live smokes by default

durable cold-start situation_routes carry typed route_targets naming the owner standard, concept, skill, runtime surface, option surface, generated projection, paper module, or principle that gives the route authority
route smoke test passes in the explicit live-smoke proof lane
negative route smoke passes in the explicit live-smoke proof lane when the route has a known nearby false-positive class
any delivered situation_route that exposes match_tokens carries a semantic contrast smoke so projected classifier anchors cannot silently overmatch adjacent navigation work

smoke_runtime_policy.reason: Actor receipt is a closeout/control-plane surface; it must not run multiple cold-start kernel subprocesses by default. Default receipt proves static delivery and records smoke configuration/deferment; explicit live-smoke proof owns latency-bearing route execution.

optional_negative_smoke_fields.smoke_negative_command: Entry or context-pack command for a nearby task that must not resolve to this route.
optional_negative_smoke_fields.smoke_forbidden_tokens: Tokens that must be absent from the negative-smoke stdout, usually the specialized situation_id.

semantic_contrast_rule.applies_when: actor_delivery.decision == deliver_to_cold_start and situation_routes[].match_tokens is non-empty
semantic_contrast_rule.failure_mode: A new projected route can be visible in compressed surfaces but classify neighboring generic navigation/projection work because only positive anchors were tested.

route_target_floor.applies_when: actor_delivery.decision == deliver_to_cold_start and situation_routes[].route_command is present
route_target_floor.relation_coverage_rule: The route target graph must include at least one authority/explanation edge (governs, explains, evidences) and at least one operational/proof edge (implements, routes_to, validates, audits, projects), so a cold agent can tell why the route is authorized and which real substrate contact proves or executes it.
route_target_floor.contact_command_rule: Targets whose relation is routes_to, validates, or audits must include command. Implements/projects targets may resolve by file/ref alone when the substrate contact is the source artifact rather than an action.
route_target_floor.resolution_rule: Each route target must resolve through its kind-specific authority plane: file-backed targets must exist as repo-relative files; concept, skill, paper_module, principle, and option_surface refs must exist in their owning registries or generated kind surfaces. Target descriptors must use the typed relation vocabulary from typed_route_target_contract.
route_target_floor.failure_mode: A cold-start route is delivered to actors as a command/prose row but does not expose the typed owner graph needed for later classifiers, projections, and mutation boundaries to agree on why that route, not merely some route, is authorized.

if_deliver_to_cold_start.projection_shape: Generated situation-route rows with actor_delivery.decision == deliver_to_cold_start should render the route, canonical next read, freshness command, and any authored fallback_command / standard_anchor so actor_delivery.required_tokens can be proven from cold-read markdown without dumping full route contracts.

required: safe drilldown route or context-pack boundary
visibility boundary such as context_pack_only, drilldown_only, private_root_only, or debug_only
defer_with_workitem capture when delivery is intended but blocked
boundary that explains why the route is not delivered now

actor_delivery_contract.repair_path: Add or refine actor_delivery on the owning situation_route in codex/doctrine/agent_bootstrap.json, project the compact Rosetta with build_agent_bootstrap_projection.py, then prove delivery with --actor-receipt. If cold-start delivery is intentionally out of scope, record context_pack_only, drilldown_only, private_root_only, debug_only, or defer_with_workitem with a reason and WorkItem ref when deferred.
anti_patterns: Treating a generated artifact as complete while no cold actor receives a route to it.
Adding every useful surface to cold-start prose instead of adding a compact Rosetta only when delivery is required.
Documenting the route only in navigation_seed.md and calling that actor delivery.
Relying on the operator to remember to ask whether a substrate reached actors.

judgment_trait_entry_rule.rule: Durable Type A judgment traits become boot-visible as compressed route pointers, never as copied philosophy or adapter-only prose.
required_surfaces: system vocabulary term row with source refs and relationships
documentation route that resolves ordinary-language queries
owning skill, standard, paper module, or axiom-candidate surface
freshness or evidence command that a cold agent can run
judgment_trait_entry_rule.growth_rule: Care and passion are initial examples, not a closed set. New traits become boot-visible only after a local case proves the route gap and a vocabulary row plus route can carry the generalized lesson.
judgment_trait_entry_rule.name_normalization_rule: Speech-to-text-corrupted, misheard, or otherwise noisy operator phrases are transcript-repair hypotheses, not negative proof or durable new labels. Before declaring a named judgment trait, register, prompt, or surface absent, repair the phrase against nearby vocabulary terms, motifs, sibling route rows, and phonetic/semantic neighbors; when a coherent local phrase such as care and passion is available, prefer that repaired phrase over preserving the corrupted token. Exact-name absence only becomes a gap after the repaired local motif route is also absent.
judgment_trait_entry_rule.adapter_shape: At most one entry affordance row may name the trait surface; details stay in the routed vocabulary, skill, standard, paper module, or axiom-candidate artifact.

up_propagation_rules: When a durable subsystem, skill, standard, paper module, or navigation layer affects cold-agent orientation, add or update an entry affordance through codex/doctrine/agent_bootstrap.json.
Do not hand-edit generated regions in AGENTS.override.md, AGENTS.md, CODEX.md, or CLAUDE.md.
Keep actor adapters actor-local: Codex deltas live in CODEX.md, Claude deltas live in CLAUDE.md, shared entry doctrine lives in AGENTS.md, and compact discovery facts live in AGENTS.override.md.
Dynamic facts must be builder-projected. If a bootstrap check reports stale live facts, run the bootstrap builder before final validation.

When a cold-agent route miss exposes an ordinary phrase for an existing doctrine cluster, repair the route alias or bootstrap affordance and point to the owning artifact instead of adding new adapter prose.
If the entry affordance needs more than one adapter row, create or update the owning paper module, skill, or standard instead.

When a durable navigation, comprehension, protocol, generated artifact, standard, skill, or checker affects agent navigation, record actor_delivery in codex/doctrine/agent_bootstrap.json. deliver_to_cold_start requires a compact Rosetta plus --actor-receipt proof; non-delivery decisions require an explicit boundary and reason.

When a Type A judgment or register trait affects cold-agent behavior, first make the ordinary noun route through system vocabulary and docs-route, then add a bootstrap situation/minimum-read-set pointer if it must be visible at boot.

When a surface is over its byte budget, apply the standard_refinement_protocol in compression_via_projection_contract: refine the standard governing the surface to enable deeper substrate-derived projection, then compress the surface to a Rosetta Stone seed. The destination is artifact-kind-dependent (paper module / skill / standard / generated region / sidecar) — pick by what the content IS, not by what is convenient.

compression_budgets.comment: Byte-budget contract per surface. Audit runtime enforces these via tools/meta/factory/check_agent_bootstrap_projection.py (byte-budget check landed 2026-04-25). Budgets are derived from project-doc discovery limits + AI-readable load cost + load-bearing builder-projected region sizes. The 2026-05-05 pri_131 update applies a governed 10% local leeway after pri_121 compression/projection checks showed reserve pressure rather than archive bloat. AGENTS.md gets the largest because it carries three builder-projected regions (live context block, paper-module index, skill catalog) plus shared route tables that all agents consume. AGENTS.override.md gets the smallest because it is the always-auto-discovered Codex seed.
