validation_rules: Every generated entry-surface policy row must cite the owning paper module or standard.
Adapter projections must keep the policy in compact form.
The compact Codex discovery seed must not exceed its configured budget.

Generated-region drift is repaired by the owning builder, not manual edits. Entry-surface diagnostics must report marker balance and renderer-content sync for managed regions, including AGENTS/CODEX/CLAUDE bootstrap blocks, instruction-discovery, paper-module index blocks, and the generated routing block.

Generated routing projection checks must expose source-worktree coupling: a routing artifact that matches the current renderer is still visible debt for scoped landing when its source_paths are dirty and the generated targets would be committed without those source inputs.

Cold routing summaries that say a generated projection is current must also surface unsafe source-worktree coupling when present; pulse, navigation metabolism, and entry-surface diagnostics must not collapse current-renderer match into landing-safe status.

Entry packets for projection-sensitive lanes (navigation/projection control, projection-closure audit, dissemination entry, and dissemination authoring) must surface entry-surface projection health from recognized_situation/selected_lane structural context, not only from diagnostic query-token triggers. Structural-only routine packets may defer full renderer-content diffing if they still report marker balance, routing source-coupling status, deferred-check fields, and the deeper checker command. Deferred renderer-content facts must be encoded as null/`deferred`, not false, so unknown is not misreported as confirmed drift.

Context-packs that select projection-sensitive stable rows (for example the dissemination skill family) must carry entry-surface projection health from selected_rows structural context, not only from diagnostic query-token triggers. Structural-only routine context-packs must use the same fast source-coupling receipt contract instead of running heavyweight renderer diffing as a side effect of row selection, and must distinguish deferred renderer-match evidence from negative evidence.

Every new durable capability, projection, evaluator, provider lane, cockpit affordance, WorkItem class, standard, or generated artifact that is meant to change agent behavior must carry an entry-adoption proof or an explicit non-entry-routable boundary per entry_adoption_contract.
The entrypoint audit must expose any missing actor coverage after entry-surface changes.
Type A judgment affordances must not exist only as adapter prose; they must resolve through `--term` and `--docs-route`.
Boot-visible judgment routes must keep the trait list open-ended and source-backed rather than freezing care and passion as the whole surface.

Every surface in compression_budgets must be at or under its budget_bytes. Audit reports surface_over_budget when a surface exceeds; repair is via standard_refinement_protocol (compression_via_projection_contract).

Adapter prose must not contain forbidden_payload patterns from surface_profiles (subsystem protocol bodies, full doctrine excerpts, actor-specific deltas in shared hub, etc). Audit reports forbidden_payload_in_adapter when detected; repair is via Rosetta Stone compression to the proper destination_kind.

When a surface is over budget AND the surface_profiles allowed_payload list does not name 'projected' content classes that could compress it, the audit must report standard_too_loose and direct the repair to refine the standard before compressing the surface.

Generated cold-agent entry surfaces must route to live compressed projections before naming bespoke trigger flags (per pri_128 — Live Projections Must Be Entry Routes). Audit reports entry_surface_missing_canonical_option_surface_route when the rendered live region or any markdown_target body omits the canonical route tokens declared in canonical_option_surface_routes.

Every populated primary scan row from the Kind Atlas-declared option surface for any kind reported by --kind-atlas must expose a non-empty `claim` field. For high-cardinality kinds this is the cluster_flag contents-page row; for smaller kinds this remains the flag row. Audit reports entry_surface_option_surface_row_lacks_claim when a populated kind's sample row is missing claim (per pri_128 canonical_option_surface_row_shape).

Every situation_route with actor_delivery.decision == deliver_to_cold_start must be reported by check_agent_bootstrap_projection.py --actor-receipt with required actors, required tokens, budget status, route smoke configuration/deferment status when configured, optional negative smoke configuration/deferment status when configured, semantic_contrast rows for projected match_tokens, and blockers for missing delivery or missing required contrast. Live smoke verdicts are owned by the explicit --actor-receipt-run-smokes proof lane so closeout receipts stay fast.

Every delivered cold-start route_target graph must carry typed descriptors (role/relation/label), relation coverage across both authority/explanation and operational/proof edges, and command-bearing contact targets for routes_to/validates/audits relations. Audit reports route_target_relation_coverage_missing when a route exposes targets but not a sufficient semantic relation graph, and route_target_contact_command_missing when a contact/proof target does not name the authorized command.

Every architectural constraint encoded in this standard (byte budget, max_adapter_rows, first_move_contract, required_tokens, row_shape rule, allowed/forbidden_payload list, threshold/count/order entry) should carry a `justifying_principle` field naming the principle that compresses its rationale. Audit reports architectural_constraint_lacks_principle_anchor at severity warning during the migration window (per pri_130 architectural_constraint_rationale_contract). Existing rows with only prose `rationale` remain valid until migrated; new constraints must include `justifying_principle` at authoring time.

canonical_option_surface_routes.comment: Operationalises pri_128. Token presence in cold-agent entry surfaces is necessary; route ordering (russian_doll first, bespoke flags as drilldowns) is the load-bearing semantic. The audit checks both layers.
canonical_option_surface_routes.principle_title: Live Projections Must Be Entry Routes

override_md_policy.reason: AGENTS.override.md has the smallest budget (17600 bytes after the governed pri_131 10% leeway) and is the always-auto-discovered Codex compact discovery seed. Per the surface_profiles allowed_payload, it carries 'one compressed entry-surface propagation pointer' to AGENTS.md and the dynamic facts. The canonical russian-doll route lives in AGENTS.md; AGENTS.override.md routes the cold agent there.
override_md_policy.fallback_pointer: AGENTS.md carries the canonical route; AGENTS.override.md must keep its propagation pointer to AGENTS.md.

bootstrap_live_state_prelude.rule: `./repo-python kernel.py --info`, `./repo-python kernel.py --preflight`, and `./repo-python kernel.py --pulse` are cheap live-state/bootstrap checks. They may precede the task control packet at session start or context recovery, but they do not choose the task lane and must not replace `--entry "<task>"` for substantive unknown work.

host_support_fast_lane.rule: Clearly external host/OS/app support tasks that do not require repo substrate, generated state, phase context, or durable doctrine edits do not require the full live-state prelude. After the compact entry seed identifies the request as outside repo authority, the agent may use the relevant local tool, OS observation, or web evidence directly and run the repo entry ladder only if the task crosses back into repo work.
applies_when: local Mac or OS setting support
installed app or browser state support
peripheral/device troubleshooting
one-off host commands whose result is the requested answer
escalate_to_repo_ladder_when: the task asks to mutate repo files, docs, standards, skills, generated projections, ledgers, or phase state
the host issue is being generalized into ai_workflow doctrine or navigation behavior
the fix requires checking current WorkItems, active phase, or repo runtime state
the agent is uncertain whether the request is external support or repo-substrate work
host_support_fast_lane.failure_prevented: Host-support tasks wasting time and context on repo live-state prelude commands before obvious OS/tool evidence.

generated_routing_projection_rule.rule: The generated AGENTS.md routing block must render the live-state prelude before `--entry` and must not introduce a pulse-first shortcut. Its Entry Protocol is an entry-surface projection, not an independent bootstrap policy.

first_move_contract.rule: Coverage is not permission: coverage proves the surface exists; it does not grant the agent permission to start there. Only CONTROL_ENTRY surfaces may select repo task control flow. For repo-substrate tasks, or at session start/context recovery when current repo state could matter, the bootstrap live-state prelude may run './repo-python kernel.py --info', './repo-python kernel.py --preflight', and './repo-python kernel.py --pulse' before the task control packet; those checks expose static/router, agent-start, and live-state facts, not the task lane. Clearly external host/OS/app support may use `host_support_fast_lane` instead of paying the full prelude. Loaded entry surfaces MUST expose './repo-python kernel.py --entry "<task>" --context-budget 12000' as the canonical task-routing front door and './repo-python kernel.py --workitem-entrypoint <phase>' for active WorkItem execution posture. After entry, unknown-kind navigation may use './repo-python kernel.py --context-pack "<task>" --context-budget 12000' as the cross-kind packet or './repo-python kernel.py --kind-atlas --band flag' as the typed contents page; './repo-python kernel.py --navigation-metabolism "<typed failure or repair id>" --context-budget 12000' is an audit/repair surface after a failed command, anti-pattern id, repair class, or owner surface is known; do not use it as a prose understanding engine. './repo-python kernel.py --option-surface <kind> --band cluster_flag' is the ATLAS_PROJECTION drilldown after Kind Atlas selects a kind or the operator explicitly asks to browse. In codex/doctrine/agent_bootstrap.json::bootstrap_sequence, entry MUST appear before context_pack, navigation_metabolism, kind_atlas, and any step whose command names a bespoke trigger flag (--paper-module, --skill-find, --annex-inspiration, --docs-route, --navigate); context_pack and navigation_metabolism are control/fallback branches, while bespoke trigger flag steps remain after kind_atlas. The bootstrap_sequence step that owns 'paper_modules' or 'skills' navigation MUST use option-surface coverage only as labeled atlas drilldown (with --paper-module <slug>, --paper-lattice navigation_hologram_theory, and --skill-find <exact> --debug only after a stable row is selected).
first_move_contract.rationale: pri_128 is no longer only 'these strings appear somewhere' or 'kind atlas before bespoke flags'; the practical repo cold-start invariant is a two-plane handshake: live-state prelude (`--info`, `--preflight`, `--pulse`) answers where the repo is when repo state matters, then the agent receives a task-conditioned control packet from `--entry`, then uses context-pack, Kind Atlas, or navigation-metabolism according to the selected lane. External host support is outside that repo control plane until it creates repo substrate work. Docs-route, skill-find, paper-module, paper-lattice, navigate, and source evidence are downstream of a selected kind, row, or typed failure.
coverage_ladder: --info then --preflight then --pulse as bootstrap live-state prelude when current state matters
--kind-atlas --band flag (ATLAS_PROJECTION after entry or explicit operator browse)
--option-surface <kind> --band cluster_flag (ATLAS_PROJECTION after Kind Atlas selects a kind)
--context-pack "<task>" only for cross-kind packets after the atlas hop
--navigation-metabolism "<typed failure or repair id>" only after command/anti-pattern/owner evidence
first_move_contract.skill_find_policy: --skill-find is DEBUG_TRACE only. Default output must not return ranked matches; scores/matched_on/token_overlap require --debug after a stable skill row or explicit operator debug request.
first_move_contract.demoted_first_move_audit_failure_reason: A bespoke trigger flag still occupies a cold-agent first-move position before the russian-doll option-surface route or kind_atlas step.

canonical_option_surface_routes.audit_failure_reason: The compressed projection substrate exists but is not visible to cold agents.
canonical_option_surface_routes.repair_path: Add or refine a situation_route in codex/doctrine/agent_bootstrap.json that names the canonical russian-doll route (--kind-atlas → --option-surface <kind_id> --band flag → --option-surface <kind_id> --band card --ids <id>) plus --navigation-context-rosetta as freshness command, then run the bootstrap builder.

canonical_option_surface_row_shape.comment: Operationalises pri_128 at row grain. canonical_option_surface_routes ensures cold agents are TOLD about the russian-doll; canonical_option_surface_row_shape ensures the rows they land on are CROSS-KIND SCANNABLE. Without a uniform one-line claim field, cross-kind scanning through the Kind Atlas-declared primary scan surface falls back to kind-specific field discovery (flag / one_liner / formal_clause / summary / purpose / statement / one_sentence_description) and the russian-doll loses its scan advantage.
canonical_option_surface_row_shape.principle_title: Live Projections Must Be Entry Routes
canonical_option_surface_row_shape.rule: Every populated primary scan row emitted by the Kind Atlas-declared `option_surface_command` for any kind reported by `./repo-python kernel.py --kind-atlas` MUST expose a non-empty string `claim` field. For high-cardinality kinds the primary scan band is `cluster_flag`; for small kinds it remains `flag`. Native kind-specific fields remain alongside; `claim` is an additive cross-kind alias.
canonical_option_surface_row_shape.audit_method: For each kind in --kind-atlas, run the declared option_surface_command scan band (`cluster_flag` when the kind's all-row flag route is a compatibility shim, otherwise `flag`). If the row set is empty or the kind does not support option-surface, skip. Otherwise inspect the first row; if `claim` is missing or empty/whitespace, fail.
canonical_option_surface_row_shape.audit_failure_reason: A populated primary scan row for a Kind Atlas kind does not carry a non-empty `claim` field, breaking cross-kind cold-agent scannability.
canonical_option_surface_row_shape.repair_path: Add a `claim` key to the affected kind's primary scan row builder in `system/lib/standard_option_surface.py`, sourcing the value from the kind's existing canonical one-line surface text (statement / formal_clause / flag / summary / one_liner / purpose / one_sentence_description). Native kind-specific fields stay; `claim` is purely additive.
non_goals: This contract enforces presence of `claim`, not semantic quality of `claim`. Some kinds (frontend_components, python_scopes) may have metadata-thin claims sourced from upstream-thin substrate; that is a quality issue tracked separately, not a presence violation.
Sampling the first row is the durable minimum. Future quality contracts may sample more rows or assert claim character minima.

architectural_constraint_rationale_contr.comment: Operationalises pri_130 (Architectural Decisions Require Principle-Anchored Justification) inside this standard. Architectural constraints affecting cold-agent entry behavior — byte budgets, line budgets, route-order rules, first-move contracts, required tokens, checker gates, forbidden_payload patterns, max_adapter_rows, etc. — must be principle-anchored, standard-encoded, and skill-operable. Existing rows may keep prose `rationale` temporarily; the migration to structured fields is forward-compatible (new fields are additive, prose stays valid until each row is migrated). Audit-level enforcement is intentionally Warning-not-Error during the migration window.
architectural_constraint_rationale_contr.rule: Every architectural constraint encoded in this standard (or any sibling standard governing agent/substrate behavior) MUST be traceable to a principle. The principle names the failure mode the constraint prevents, the bound the constraint respects, and the substrate evidence behind the choice. The constraint's row in the standard MUST cite the principle via a `justifying_principle` field, and the operational practice for applying / questioning / repairing the constraint MUST live in a skill named via `governing_skill`.
applies_to_field_kinds: validation_rules entries that encode threshold / count / order rules
checker gate constants in tools/meta/factory/check_*.py that mirror these standard fields

required_decision_fields.justifying_principle: Stable principle id (e.g. `pri_131`) that names the WHY for this constraint. The principle row must exist in raw_seed_principles.json and must surface via `--option-surface principles --band card --ids <principle_id>`.
required_decision_fields.failure_prevented: One sentence naming the cold-agent / substrate / operator failure mode the constraint prevents. Example: 'cold-agent first-read truncation when AGENTS.md exceeds the host harness instruction budget'.
required_decision_fields.evidence_basis: Where the bound's specific number / shape comes from: external spec (e.g. Codex `project_doc_max_bytes` default), measured cold-agent attention curve, historical operator decision with par_* anchor, or substrate-derived computation. Prose alone is not sufficient; the field should name the source.
required_decision_fields.change_protocol: When and how the constraint may be revised. Examples: 'requires principle update + standard update + checker pass', 'requires operator approval via raw-seed append', 'auto-derived from substrate; mutates only via the projector'.
required_decision_fields.review_command: The kernel command an agent runs to verify the constraint is currently satisfied or to inspect the substrate basis. Example: `./repo-python tools/meta/factory/check_agent_bootstrap_projection.py`.
required_decision_fields.repair_path: Short pointer to the operational practice: 'apply pri_121 compression before raising the budget', 'route prose to local_to_general_propagation.md before adding to AGENTS.md', etc.

architectural_constraint_rationale_contr.anti_pattern: A numeric cap, route-order rule, or structural default exists only as a value plus prose `rationale`, with no `justifying_principle` anchor and no named operational skill path. Reading the standard reveals the number but not the WHY behind it; questioning the number cannot be done from doctrine because no principle compresses the rationale.
architectural_constraint_rationale_contr.default_repair: When a constraint is questioned or exceeded: (a) apply the existing repair path (e.g. pri_121 compression before raising a byte budget); (b) if no principle anchor exists, mint or update a principle that names the WHY, then add `justifying_principle` to the constraint row; (c) update the governing_skill so agents have a named practice for this class of constraint. Do not raise the constraint as the first move.
architectural_constraint_rationale_contr.audit_failure_reason: An architectural constraint encoded in std_agent_entry_surface.json (or sibling) has no `justifying_principle` field, breaking the principle → standard → skill traceability that pri_130 requires. The constraint may still be load-bearing locally, but its rationale is not principle-traceable, which prevents auditable change.
non_goals: This contract does NOT require every constant in code to carry a principle anchor — the scope is explicitly architectural constraints affecting agent or substrate behavior, not internal implementation defaults. pri_130 failure_modes[5] names the over-correction failure mode.
Migration is intentionally non-breaking: existing rows with only prose `rationale` remain valid until migrated. The audit failure is severity Warning, not Error, during the migration window.
This contract does not specify which principle must justify which constraint; that is a per-constraint decision that the principle-mint process owns.
named_followons_inherited_from_pri_130: Historical pri_130 follow-on: anchor the former AGENTS.md byte cap in a child principle. Closed by pri_131; future cap changes must update pri_131 evidence/local_basis plus compression_budgets and checker coherence.

Mint a sibling principle for the asymmetric AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md adapter reading topology operator flagged the same turn pri_130 was minted (likely shape: 'Shared doctrine must reach every adapter reading path').
Anchor pri_128 to a real par_* (still session_discovery + machine_telemetry + principle_relation; mirror the pri_129 anchor pattern from commit 6ee9d83c).

cross_surface_capability_federation_cont.purpose: Entry surfaces must federate capabilities across repo doctrine skills, project Agent Skills, user/global Agent Skills, Codex user/legacy skill paths, Claude plugin skills, MCP tools, harness session-advertised skills, annex references, and public standards without collapsing their authority lanes.
cross_surface_capability_federation_cont.rule: Availability claims require evidence. Source presence, registry status, publication topology, session advertisement, and successful invocation are distinct states. Generated capability projections are projections, not source authority.
applies_when: any task touches a domain whose capabilities are carried by more than one of: repo doctrine skills, Agent Skills, plugin skills, MCP tools, annex references, public standards
an entry packet is asked to recognize a domain where the kernel projection is structurally blind to harness/user/plugin lanes
a generated capability surface is being authored or refreshed
anti_patterns: treating source presence as invocation availability
treating generated projections as source authority
treating session-advertised skills as durable source truth
adding external plugin/tool carriers as local skill rows without an explicit mirror standard
hand-maintaining separate Codex and Claude capability lists
collapsing MCP tools, skills, annexes, and standards into one skill bucket
patching skill frontmatter before the entry surface route is fixed
promoting annexes blindly without per-annex disposition (reuse/finish/import/bridge/build)
cross_surface_capability_federation_cont.source_authority_boundary: source-controlled authored seed != runtime/generated projection. Routes/MRS must read the seed when no builder exists; runtime projections are derived artifacts that may add live availability checks but cannot be the only canonical read.
